summary: Targeted host-keyed RCE backdoor in TypeScript linter package
details: On require(), index.js loads lib/perf.js which reads /etc/machine-id, uses those bytes as an AES-128-CBC key (with a hardcoded IV) to decrypt a ~4KB hex-encoded blob, and passes the plaintext to `new Function(r)()` for execution. The module exports `{}` and is named 'perf' to masquerade as a benign utility. Because the decryption key is the host's machine-id, the payload only resolves to runnable code on one or a small set of pre-targeted machines — the canonical targeted supply-chain backdoor shape. The package self-describes as a TypeScript naming-convention linter and has no legitimate need for cryptographic operations, host-identifier reads, or dynamic code execution. Any consumer that imports this package on a targeted host executes attacker-controlled code with full Node process privileges; on non-targeted hosts the decrypt fails silently inside the try/catch, hiding the mechanism from casual review.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
